name: A-140
description: Classic ADSR envelope generator. Gate input triggers envelope; Retrig re-opens attack while gate held. Two identical positive outputs (~0 to +8V) plus an inverted output (~0 to -8V). 3-position time range switch spans <100µs to minutes.

controls.name: A
controls.description: Attack time — rise from 0V to peak (~+8V) after gate on.
controls.detail: L: sub-ms · M: standard · H: up to minutes (set by Time Range)

controls.name: D
controls.description: Decay time — fall from peak to sustain level.
controls.detail: L: sub-ms · M: standard · H: up to minutes (set by Time Range)

controls.name: S
controls.description: Sustain level — steady voltage held while gate remains high.

controls.name: R
controls.description: Release time — fall from sustain to 0V after gate off.
controls.detail: L: sub-ms · M: standard · H: up to minutes (set by Time Range)

controls.name: Time Range
controls.description: 3-position range switch scales all four time stages (A, D, R).
controls.detail: L: <100µs–fast · M: standard mid-range · H: up to minutes

controls.name: ADSR Control
controls.description: LED tracks envelope output voltage for visual feedback.

inputs.name: Gate
inputs.description: Gate input. Rising edge starts A→D→S phase; falling edge starts R phase.
inputs.normalizedTo: A-100 bus INT.GATE line (disable via internal red jumper)

inputs.name: Retrig
inputs.description: Re-trigger input. Pulse restarts envelope from current level while Gate is still high.

outputs.name: Output
outputs.description: Second identical positive envelope output (hard-wired copy, no buffer).

behaviors.name: Standard ADSR shape
behaviors.description: On gate rising edge, output rises to peak over A time, falls to S level over D time, holds at S until gate off, then falls to 0V over R time.

behaviors.name: Gate-length release
behaviors.trigger: short gate during A or D
behaviors.description: If gate falls before sustain is reached, envelope begins R from the current voltage level, producing shortened percussive shapes.

behaviors.name: Re-triggering
behaviors.description: Each Retrig pulse restarts A→D from current envelope value without requiring gate to cycle. Driving Retrig from an LFO produces repeating envelopes locked to the gate duration.

behaviors.name: System bus gate normalling
behaviors.description: Gate jack is normalled to the A-100 system bus INT.GATE line, so MIDI-CV or keyboard gates reach all A-140 modules without patching. Insert a cable to break the bus normal.

behaviors.name: Bipolar modulation from Inverse Out
behaviors.description: Inverse Output carries the same shape mirrored to negative voltages, useful for simultaneous opening/closing of paired VCAs or crossfader duck patches.

patchIdeas.name: Classic voice envelope
patchIdeas.patch: Gate → Gate · Output → VCA CV · Inverse Output → VCF CV for sweep-down
patchIdeas.diagram:
A-140:
* A: fast · D: med · S: 6 · R: med · Time Range: M
- Keyboard (Gate) g> A-140 (Gate)
- A-140 (Output) >> VCA (CV)
- A-140 (Inverse Output) >> VCF (CV) // filter closes as envelope opens

patchIdeas.name: Retrig-driven tremolo envelope
patchIdeas.patch: LFO square → Retrig repeats A/D bursts while key held · Output → VCA
patchIdeas.diagram:
A-140:
* A: fast · D: slow · S: 0 · R: med · Time Range: L
- Keyboard (Gate) g> A-140 (Gate)
- LFO (Sqr) t> A-140 (Retrig)
- A-140 (Output) >> VCA (CV) // percussive bursts inside each note

patchIdeas.name: Dual destination from twin outputs
patchIdeas.patch: Both positive outputs drive independent targets without a mult — one to VCA, one to pitch FM
patchIdeas.diagram:
A-140:
* A: slow · D: med · S: 4 · R: slow
- Gate Source (Gate) g> A-140 (Gate)
- A-140 (Output) >> VCA (CV)
- A-140 (Output) >> VCO (Exp FM) // small pitch rise on attack

patchIdeas.name: Ducking sidechain
patchIdeas.patch: Drum gate → Gate · Inverse Output → VCA CV on pad voice for rhythmic duck
patchIdeas.diagram:
A-140:
* A: very fast · D: med · S: 0 · R: med
- Drum (Gate) g> A-140 (Gate)
- Pad Voice (Audio) -> VCA (In)
- A-140 (Inverse Output) >> VCA (CV) // VCA dips on each hit

patchIdeas.name: Slow drone morph
patchIdeas.patch: Time Range H for minute-long sweeps · Output → filter cutoff for slowly evolving timbre
patchIdeas.diagram:
A-140:
* A: 10 · D: 10 · S: 5 · R: 10 · Time Range: H
- Manual Gate (Gate) g> A-140 (Gate)
- A-140 (Output) >> VCF (Cutoff) // multi-minute opening
